zh.lifeSummary: 當George Campbell 13th of Loudoun於1420年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾盧度時，他的父親Sir George Campbell 12th of Loudoun31歲，母親Katherine Kennedy of Cassillis20歲。他於1444年3月29日與Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley在蘇格蘭艾斯爾盧度結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1491年3月9日在蘇格蘭艾斯爾盧頓享年71歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当George Campbell 13th of Loudoun于1420年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾盧度时，他的父亲Sir George Campbell 12th of Loudoun31岁，母亲Katherine Kennedy of Cassillis20岁。他于1444年3月29日与Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley在苏格兰艾斯爾盧度结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。他去世于1491年3月9日在苏格兰艾斯爾盧頓享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

en.lifeSummary: When George Campbell 13th of Loudoun was born in 1420, in Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland, his father, Sir George Campbell 12th of Loudoun, was 31 and his mother, Katherine Kennedy of Cassillis, was 20. He married Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley on 29 March 1444, in Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died on 9 March 1491, in Loudon, Ayrshire, Scotland, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque George Campbell 13th of Loudoun est né en 1420, à Loudoun, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Sir George Campbell 12th of Loudoun, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Katherine Kennedy of Cassillis, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley le 29 mars 1444, à Loudoun, Ayrshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé le 9 mars 1491, à Loudon, Ayrshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als George Campbell 13th of Loudoun 1420 in Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir George Campbell 12th of Loudoun 31 und seine Mutter Katherine Kennedy of Cassillis 20. Er hat am 29. März 1444 in Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb am 9. März 1491 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Loudon, Ayrshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando George Campbell 13th of Loudoun è nato il nel 1420 nel Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir George Campbell 12th of Loudoun, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Katherine Kennedy of Cassillis, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley il 29 marzo 1444, nel Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì il 9 marzo 1491, nel Loudon, Ayrshire, Scotland, all'età di 71 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: George Campbell 13th of Loudounが1420年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアロウドウンで生まれた時，父Sir George Campbell 12th of Loudounは31歳，母Katherine Kennedy of Cassillisは20歳であった。1444年3月29日にスコットランドエイルシュアイアロウドウンでElizabeth Stewart of Darnleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。彼は1491年3月9日にスコットランドエイルシュアイアロウドンで71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: George Campbell 13th of Loudoun(이)가 1420년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 루다운에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir George Campbell 12th of Loudoun(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Kennedy of Cassillis(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1444년 3월 29일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 루다운에서 Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1491년 3월 9일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 루던에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando George Campbell 13th of Loudoun nasceu em 1420, em Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir George Campbell 12th of Loudoun, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Kennedy of Cassillis, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley em 29 de março de 1444, em Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Ele faleceu em 9 de março de 1491, em Loudon, Ayrshire, Scotland, com 71 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Campbell 13th of Loudoun родился в 1420 г. в местности лоудоун, аиршир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir George Campbell 12th of Loudoun было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Katherine Kennedy of Cassillis, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley 29 марта 1444 г. в местности лоудоун, аиршир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер 9 марта 1491 г. в местности лоудон, аиршир, Шотландия в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando George Campbell 13th of Loudoun nació en 1420, en Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir George Campbell 12th of Loudoun, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Katherine Kennedy of Cassillis, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley el 29 de marzo de 1444, en Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió el 9 de marzo de 1491, en Loudon, Ayrshire, Scotland, a la edad de 71 años.
